title: (JPN SUB) 너무 사랑하는 사람과는 절대 행복할 수 없습니다
text: This might be a bit hard to understand, but I feel like you can't date someone for a long time with the person you ideally love too much. When I just say that, the number of people who relate is... I don't know. You know, I love that person so much from a rational perspective. If people in the field of dating talk about getting married this year, I'll bring that up. When I say that if you love someone too much, you can't date for long and the ending isn't good, I mean that we need to break down the concept of love a bit. It involves sleeping together, going on dates, having daily conversations, and so on—there are probably many levels if you look closely. But if, without missing a single one of those emotions, you feel like "it has to be that person," or "if it's that person, everything is unconditionally good." You know, that feeling of loving someone so much that you couldn't put them in your throat even if a knife were held to your throat. Someone to the point where you can't realistically distinguish between them. To put it simply, if you love that person to the point where you feel like you've contracted an incurable disease, or that you love them enough to tear out your heart—if you are feeling that way right now, exactly at that stage, then you can't date that person for long. You might ask, "If the other person loves me just as much as I love them, isn't that okay?" But even then, it doesn't work for most people. If I love someone that much, the other person will feel less affection. This is because, instinctively, if you feel you are receiving an undeserved amount of love, no one will go out of their way to give more. You get used to receiving it and think, "I should at least give a little more." Generally speaking, if we consider the current situation, when you view and love someone too ideally, you will face many situations where it is realistically impossible to distinguish between the two. You feel you can do anything the other person wants, no matter what is your fault. In such a situation, not only is the other person affected, but you yourself cannot help but get exhausted. Next, let me talk about the opposite. When I speak of a love that seems like it can last a long time, it is the

title: (JPN SUB) 너무 사랑하는 사람과는 절대 행복할 수 없습니다
text: what is your fault. In such a situation, not only is the other person affected, but you yourself cannot help but get exhausted. Next, let me talk about the opposite. When I speak of a love that seems like it can last a long time, it is the feeling of, "If I break up with this person, it will be incredibly difficult, but I still have to live." I understand that feeling. You think, "I have to live diligently, if only to keep the promise I made when we broke up." The love I am talking about is this kind of love. On the other hand, when you are saying "It's hard, it's hard," the kind of love where you think, "If this person is n't here, I am too. It would be better to die." To summarize in one word, if you are in love like the latter, you can easily see the end of it; it wo n't last long. It’s like saying I’ll have to do it someday. As I’ve said, breaking up is heartbreaking and difficult, but if I have the thought that I should live my life diligently for that person's sake, I believe that if you are that kind of person, you can live happily for a long time. Please think about it for a moment: are you truly a human being when you are acting this way? Lately, I’ve been sending a lot of emails and KakaoTalk messages. Even though I have written down below the notifications on KakaoTalk that I am not accepting personal counseling, and even during broadcasts, I keep looking at emails. They start with things like, "I know you are receiving this, but it’s late, and I know you trust me, but I’m really having such a hard time. I have nowhere else to contact, and you are the only person I can think of." I get so many messages like this every day. But I can't reply to every single one because there are just too many. However, when I read them, I find that there is always at least one common thread. And the thought that comes to mind is that I’m seeing my old self. So whenever I feel that, whenever you talk about things like being unemployed or feeling broken—the things you commonly say—I think to myself, "I had those days too, I had times like that." But no matter what, a normal person... using their own standards as a yardstick... a third party, or... me, at this time, what, or my You know how friends generally treat people they know like this on the phone? But there are actually a lot of people seeking counseling who think, "I wasn't like this when I was older." When you look at those cases, as I mentioned earlier, there is usually one

title: (JPN SUB) 너무 사랑하는 사람과는 절대 행복할 수 없습니다
text: generally treat people they know like this on the phone? But there are actually a lot of people seeking counseling who think, "I wasn't like this when I was older." When you look at those cases, as I mentioned earlier, there is usually one commonality. Most of them like the other person too much— whether it is love or some other feeling of liking—because they don't really know, since only that person knows. The second commonality is that the other person is not a decent human being, or someone who drives you crazy. If anyone saw that the other person act this way, and I were the one involved, I would n't be able to bear it either. Most people in relationships like this are the norm. There are almost no counseling cases where people fight over trivial personality issues and then face reality right before marriage; you can consider them practically non-existent. I can't really describe the majority, but seeing that, I reminisced about my own memories. But when I thought about it, first of all, even though I said I wasn't sure if it was love or not, I was looking at that person with the premise that I just like them. You know that moment when you start liking them so much? I lost the self-control to judge things rationally, even when they did something wrong. No matter what kind of trashy things that person did, others might not know, but at least to me, while I could tell them, " This isn't right," I couldn't know anything beyond that. Even if it doesn't work out, even if it hurts, I have to meet them. I have to meet them even though I hate that about them. Even though they really did something wrong, I have to meet them because I like them. It is 100% just being dragged around. Everyone is like that in all counseling sessions. In conclusion, when people ask, "What is the problem with them? What should be done?", no matter how much I respond, saying things like "Please break up," or " Someone is at fault," they just say, "I can't hear you." There is no reason to reply, it is meaningless, because from their perspective, no matter how much someone tells them to do it, they are not someone who can listen. They have to realize that themselves. But looking back on those memories,

title: (JPN SUB) 너무 사랑하는 사람과는 절대 행복할 수 없습니다
text: " There is no reason to reply, it is meaningless, because from their perspective, no matter how much someone tells them to do it, they are not someone who can listen. They have to realize that themselves. But looking back on those memories, I felt a huge sense of defeat in the battle against myself when I lacked self-control and the ability to manage my inner feelings. In the very early stages, we met without any issues, but we would fight once in a while. At those times, I used to think, "Couples usually fight like this." Even while doing it, when you reach that point, you start thinking, "Why do I have to meet this person to this extent?" Yet, while harboring those thoughts, you fail to put them into action, and you end up in that situation. At that moment, it is as if you have lost the ability to control yourself. That is why it is a time when you cannot listen to anyone, even if they tell you; there is nothing you can do. So, from that point on, you start giving up.

title: (JPN SUB) 너무 사랑하는 사람과는 절대 행복할 수 없습니다
text: ituation. At that moment, it is as if you have lost the ability to control yourself. That is why it is a time when you cannot listen to anyone, even if they tell you; there is nothing you can do. So, from that point on, you start giving up. You create thoughts like, "It seems like something like this should never happen to the people around me," "Why am I acting like this?" "I know I should be achieving this and doing this, but I absolutely cannot." You cannot accept it. So, you start thinking, "Why on earth is this person acting this way? I have this person too, why are they doing this to me?" and you get angry at yourself. From that point on, you realize it feels like a distant feeling. You trust the other person, you are truly annoyed, and the truth is, that time has already passed. But you don't want to meet them again. What that means is that I want to see this person, but because they don't act the way I expect, I start getting annoyed. So, common behaviors like begging for an affair, making demands, or throwing objects occur because you have lost your ability to control yourself. There is no way to soothe that anger. It doesn't exist. So, it means they seek extreme forms of love, like throwing things or swearing while fighting. But having gone through that myself and looking back now, I fully understand those emotions. However, thinking about it now, you know that moment when you feel like things are going wrong? If that happens, you can consider it over. You can't turn back time; it's a finished game. If you just keep trying not to die, waiting for a comrade to come and save you, you end up dying anyway. The only person who can save you is Bonnie. It's better to just die and be reborn. That's much faster; that's the right way. In reality, dying means you can't succeed, but in a game, if you die, you are reborn. You can play a new game. And this is the time for the three resets. You must never forget the ability to control your own emotions. You have to stay in a state of tension even while dating because you never know when or where something might happen. When you develop the ability to control yourself without expressing that anger to your partner, even when a situation arises that makes you really angry, that's when both of you can stay together for a long time without any problems. Usually, when you're in a relationship, trivial We often start with a little bickering, and as it escalates, we have a lot of fights that spiral out of control. However, once you develop the self-control to manage things, the bickering naturally ends right there. If neither you nor your partner can handle it, the relationship is completely hopeless. If I can do it but my partner can't, the relationship won't last long either. Both of you need to be capable of that. You need to be able to control yourself, manage your anger, and be considerate of your partner— people who are capable of growing on their own. You probably think you can do all of that, right? We're human, after all. Why would you think it wouldn't work? Simply put, it's this: witness the person you love cheating, or see them completely drunk and acting in a way that no human being should. Even in these situations, you need to have the ability to step beyond that and maintain control. If neither of you can do that, you'll just fight at the drop of a hat, saying "you did this" or "I did that," until it escalates and ends. I had a vague feeling about it, but I feel so good because I think I finally got the answer I was looking for today. It is a truly difficult skill, but if you cultivate it well, life... It doesn't seem like you'll have a hard life. Think about it carefully.
